methods.name: addByTSMDescription
methods.description: Creates a new TSplineBody using the T-Spline description provided by the input string which contains TSM formatted text.

methods.name: addByTSMFile
methods.description: Creates a new TSplineBody by reading in a TSM file from disk.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified T-Spline body using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Returns a TSplineBody by specifying the name of the body as seen in the browser.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of bodies in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

addByTSMDescription.title: TSplineBodies.addByTSMDescription Method
addByTSMDescription.description: Creates a new TSplineBody using the T-Spline description provided by the input string which contains TSM formatted text.
addByTSMDescription.return_description: Returns the newly created TSplineBody if successful or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: tsmDescription
parameters.description: A string that contains a T-Spline description in TSM form.

addByTSMFile.title: TSplineBodies.addByTSMFile Method
addByTSMFile.description: Creates a new TSplineBody by reading in a TSM file from disk.
addByTSMFile.return_description: Returns the newly created TSplineBody if successful or null in the case of failure.

parameters.name: tsmFilename
parameters.description: The full filename of the TSM file on disk.

classType.title: TSplineBodies.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

item.title: TSplineBodies.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified T-Spline body using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: TSplineBodies.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Returns a TSplineBody by specifying the name of the body as seen in the browser.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if a body with that name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the body, as seen in the browser. This is case sensitive.

count.title: TSplineBodies.count Property
count.description: The number of bodies in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = tSplineBodies_var.count

isValid.title: TSplineBodies.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = tSplineBodies_var.isValid

objectType.title: TSplineBodies.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = tSplineBodies_var.objectType
